threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the 'Overground' instead of the 'Ginger Line'—everyone around here knows that's the real name.
Caught a bloke trying to sell homemade perfume on the 133 last night. Smelled like a mix of cheap cologne and jaffa cakes. Never know what you'll find!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a guy playing the accordion for tips while his dog politely nodded along on the corner of Weserstraße. Classic Neukölln moment.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find a kebab shop with vegan döner that slaps harder than the classical concert next door. Pure Kreuzberg magic.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they know to avoid 16th and Mission after 9 PM—it's a whole different vibe, trust me.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tux on the 38, heading to a wedding, while the bus was packed with late-night partiers from the bar. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think Gràcia’s plaza is just for selfies, but locals know it’s our living room—where we catch up and watch the world go by.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You’ll notice them squinting at maps, often stops mid-street to take a photo of the tiniest arquitectura. It's like a walking travel guide!

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still stand at the bus stop, not knowing how to hail a cab with a whip of your hand. Everyone else just raises an eyebrow.
Caught a guy on the 133 last night who was rapping his life story to the driver. Didn't skip a beat as we rolled past Electric Avenue.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw an entire family of ducks waddling down the Karl-Marx-Strasse, stopping traffic like they owned the place. Only in Neukölln!

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you see people lounging on urban playgrounds, sipping coffee while kids swing nearby, making even the swings feel like a chill hangout spot.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they automatically brace themselves as they approach BART after 5 PM, like it's a rollercoaster ride.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a man in a tuxedo casually hopping on the 19 bus at 2 AM, clutching a bouquet. Only in the Tenderloin would you see style mixed with late-night chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the street performances are spontaneous, but most have permits and schedules. The real magic is in the quieter corners where locals gather.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still trying to navigate the metro map, staring at it like it’s a puzzle, while locals rush by without a glance.
